user_query:
I’m analyzing how default OS policies around updates and telemetry might reflect broader governance choices. Using the sample configuration tree under input/, please audit it and produce three artifacts under output/:

1) output/discovered_files.txt — a newline-separated list of every file you read while completing this task, with paths relative to the workspace.
2) output/repo_list.csv — a CSV with header: type,uri,suite,components, listing all APT repositories found by parsing input/apt/sources.list (and any other *.list files under input/apt/). Include lines that start with deb or deb-src (ignore commented and blank lines). The components column should contain the remaining space-separated components joined by a single space.
3) output/system_policy_report.json — a JSON report with the following structure:
   {
     "repos": [ { "type": "deb|deb-src", "uri": string, "suite": string, "components": [string, ...] } ],
     "security_repo_present": boolean,
     "periodic_settings": {
       "Update-Package-Lists": string|number|null,
       "Download-Upgradeable-Packages": string|number|null,
       "Unattended-Upgrade": string|number|null
     },
     "telemetry_services": [ { "unit": string, "type": string|null, "exec_start": string|null, "telemetry_endpoint": string|null } ],
     "telemetry_config": { "enabled": boolean|null, "endpoint": string|null }
   }

Details and constraints:
- Recursively scan input/ to discover and read relevant files. At minimum, parse:
  • APT repo definitions from any *.list file under input/apt/ (e.g., input/apt/sources.list). Extract: type (deb or deb-src), uri, suite, and components (as an array in the JSON and a space-joined string in the CSV). Set security_repo_present to true if any repo’s suite contains "security" (e.g., focal-security).  
  • APT periodic settings from any files under input/apt/apt.conf.d/ by reading APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists, APT::Periodic::Download-Upgradeable-Packages, and APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade. If a key is not present in any file, record it as null. Keep values as strings or numbers as parsed.
  • systemd service metadata from any .service files under input/systemd/system/. For services whose Description or ExecStart suggest telemetry (e.g., contain "telemetry" case-insensitively), capture unit (filename), Type (if present), ExecStart (full command), and any TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT value from Environment lines if present.
  • Telemetry agent settings from input/telemetry/telemetry.conf (simple key = value format). Extract enabled as a boolean (true/false) and endpoint as a string if present; otherwise set to null.
- Only use the provided files; do not rely on the host system’s real /etc. All paths in discovered_files.txt must correspond to files you actually read from input/.
- The outputs must be reproducible from these inputs without Internet access.

Deliver exactly the three files at the specified paths and ensure the JSON and CSV are syntactically valid. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
